inspirationSources.application: The rectangular punch card as the atomic UI component — each card has a header row (dark bar), data fields in fixed-width columns, and a footer with metadata. The rigid geometry and reference numbering system derive from physical card formatting conventions.
Dense monospaced data readouts, system status indicators, operator greeting conventions, reference numbering on every element (STAT-001, ENT-002), and the overall information-first layout philosophy.
The data wall grid layout concept — multiple rectangular panels arranged in a rigid grid filling the full viewport, each showing a different metric or data stream. No whitespace wasted.
Zero border-radius, raw structural borders, exposed grid, monumental typography, no gradients, no rounded corners, no shadows except hard-edge drop shadows on hover.

inspirationSources.reference: IBM Plex Mono typeface design rationale
inspirationSources.application: Selected for its readability at small sizes in dense data presentations. IBM Plex was designed for technical documentation and data-heavy interfaces — directly aligned with the mainframe operator persona.
Used for headings to evoke typewriter/teletype output — the primary output device of mainframe-era computing. Gives headings an authoritative, machine-generated quality.
The top tab strip navigation is modeled on web browser tab bars — a workbench metaphor where each tab is a different view/tool, and the currently active tab appears connected to the content below.
Settings toggles display binary 0/1 values instead of conventional on/off switches, reinforcing the mainframe computing metaphor where everything reduces to binary state.

Subtle horizontal line overlay (1px at 2% opacity) across the entire viewport, referencing CRT monitor scanlines without resorting to the green-on-black terminal aesthetic that was explicitly avoided.

avoidedElements: No concrete slab or paper textures — uses flat color surfaces only
No stamp grids, numbered badges, or postal motifs — uses typed reference numbers instead
No CRT green-on-black or phosphor glow — warm off-white palette with dark borders
No blueprint grid or construction zone orange — orange is accent-only, no grid background
No torn paper, collage, or zine aesthetic — clean rectangular geometry throughout
No corrugated steel, barcode labels, hazmat stripes, or warehouse containers — data processing metaphor, not industrial

distinctionFromPriorPasses: This pass is unique through its punched-card atomic component model, browser-tab-row navigation, mainframe operator content persona, binary toggle switches, reference numbering system on all elements, and warm off-white/dark-border palette that avoids all previously used brutalist textures and motifs.
